lines.text: Office Memorandum • UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT
TO : Director, FBI
DATE: July 29, 1952
FROM : SAC, WASHINGTON FIELD 62-0
SUBJECT: AERIAL OBJECTS
"Flying Saucers"
INFORMATION CONCERNING
On July 28, 1952, at the Weekly Intelligence Conference,
Captain [REDACTED] Office of Special Investigations, 4th Air
Force Base, Bolling Field, advised the representative of the FBI,
Washington Field Office, over the prior weekend, his office had
received reports concerning the observation of aerial objects.
Captain [REDACTED] referred to a phone call made to his office by [REDACTED]
Special Agent [REDACTED] of the Washington Field Office,
furnishing to OSI the identity of a woman who reportedly had observed
an aerial object. [REDACTED] praised the WFO for the expeditious notification
in this instance.
Captain [REDACTED], Colonel [REDACTED], [REDACTED] superior office,
and Special Agent [REDACTED] in a conference on July 28,
1952, discussed the matter of immediate notification of OSI. This
conference resulted in the request by Colonel [REDACTED] and Captain
[REDACTED] that in the event, whenever information is received by the FBI,
in which it is alleged that someone has observed a "Flying Saucer",
this information be immediately relayed to Captain [REDACTED] office.
It is to be noted that this office may be reached by dialing Code 1261
and asking for Extension 509. Captain [REDACTED] said this number should be
called under these circumstances at any time, day or night.
Captain [REDACTED] stated that the Air Force is greatly concerned
about this matter of aerial objects and said that the Air Force
Intelligence would appreciate the execution of the requested procedure,
mentioned above. It is suggested that the Bureau bring this matter
to the attention of its night supervisors, since these night supervisors
also receive complaints from persons outside of the Bureau and may
receive information concerning the observation of aerial objects.
JMD:NPB
RECORDED - 59
UNRECORDED COPY FILED IN 62-[unclear]
